fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 74 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch body (clamping logic) should execute, but `if not clamp` means it executes when clamp is False instead — the condition is inverted.
The then-branch (clamping) should only execute when clamp is False due to the inverted condition, but it should execute when clamp is True.

spec.expr: y__2 >= min(y0, y1) and y__2 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: After clamping, y__2 must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].

spec.expr: not clamp or (__cbfl_result >= min(y0, y1) and __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the returned value must be within the segment's rating range.
